panels.text: Enterprise in Cloud is the current priority and will continue to receive primary resource allocation. Work on DC Exit will progress as capacity becomes available. Workshops and planning calls held to discuss scope and next steps, further planning to take place. A spreadsheet containing resources has been compiled with some quick wins identified. An initial review of HR and finance servers has been completed. A review of SFTP has been carried out. A call was held with pro2col who supply and support GoAnywhere. They could support migration and will provided licences. Migration of GoAnywhere will take place after Enterprise migration is complete. Deployments of SFTP servers will begin WC

06/07/2026 SQL MI has performance issues around use of TempDB. A SQL VM has been created in Azure UAT to compare performance against SQL MI and on prem. Initial results show increased performance compared to SQL MI. SQL VMs are being further built out in the UAT environment. Elastic consumption and spend is significantly higher than originally target. The Elastic PS is unlikely to reduce the spend to the forecast level. Dev and Test environments have been migrated to Azure. The Test migration took longer than expected and had issues. Further migrations in the Prep environment are required to prove and refine the process. RAG is Red due to outstanding work to be completed for migration.

6/7/2026 For  4crisk.ai  waiting for Supradeep to confirm when we can complete the transfer. Scheduled for 21st of July   For the 3 phigrc ones ( phigrc.com ,  phigrc.ai  and  phigrccom.onmicrosoft.com ) we are waiting for transfer details. Have chased Supradeep Acin Registrar migration will be completed next week when Giuseppe is back from leave and Acin release is completed this week.

items.summary: Operationalise Security Aggregation and Asset Management

panels.text: 06/07/26 Weekly calls being held on Wednesdays with Ash and Axonius. The Axonius Gateway VM has been created in Azure v2 and credentials for datasources provisioned.

06/07/26 All CUBE repos have been migrated to GHE and pipelines relinked to use GHE. Migration of Acin, Kodex and 4C Risk repos is to be scheduled and completed. 2 Acin repos have been migrated. These will be reviewed and the remained scheduled. Kodex to be migrated 20 and 21 July. Discussion to be held with 4C regarding migration dates. The target to complete pipeline migration is end of August. A template pipeline will be created and shared with the teams, with the initial step to create documentation for this.

items.summary: SJP - Sunset of Documentum, xPression & Transform stack

panels.text: Next steps - Agree on the project plan and create a SoW. Awaiting SJP to submit project milestones and the project status meeting cadence between now and the end of the term (March 2027), which we can use as the basis for our SOW

CustomerProfileConceptRel data cleanup on the customer database on RPG in Production completed. Completed TEST SQL MI configuration and successfully failed over to Azure. Reinstalled SQL Server 2025 on the new DRP database server but need clarification if these are still required if the architecture design as changed regarding SQL MI. Configured the UAT Azure SQL VM and all databases restored as standalone ready for testing. Ongoing tasks to build out new UAT SQL Azure VMs for go-live migration Kick off data cleanup in RGP DEV for the InsightProfileResult and CustomerInsightRel tables after script deletion review.     No progress on the RGP Test SQL Managed Instance configuration setup last week due to other priorities; work planned to resume this week. Database Watch now configured on AZURE to help with ongoing performance issues on UAT. But the DBAs are unable to view some of the dashboards due to permission issues. Raised this with Paul and waiting for response.  Successfully rolled out Query Store across all DRP production databases.  Configuration setup for the RGP Test SQL Managed Instance was meant to commence on Friday but was off sick. Will start looking at this this week Continue reviewing database access across DRP environments and progress remediation activities. Add missing databases to the PROD1 AlwaysOn Availability Group. Completed  Kick off DRP remediation activities, including planning for the migration of databases to the new server estate. Ongoing and next phase to start this week   ===    [DBOPS-169] Upgrade PROD2 Windows/SQL Servers to v2022   PROD2 Database migration to the new set of production servers, completed over the course of the weekend (02th of May 2026) [DBOPS-54] AG & Replication Monitoring Always On Alerting Implementation: Completed and running on all prod and UAT environments on the on-prem environment Outstanding:- Documenting setup [DBOPS-92] Query Optimisation - Production Ongoing works with the Engineering team to implement recommended changes. Most of the tasks have now been allocated to an engineer who is working closely with the DBA team [DBOPS-288] Enterprise Dev team access to Azure SQL MI UAT database Failover to Azure Assisting with troubleshooting on DEV SQL MI

06/07/26     Azure Migrate has been set up to assess migration of Content VMs to Azure. Work to migrate Dev and Test databases to Azure SQL MI initiated, currently paused. Calls with Microsoft Factory team held, they are able to support and carry out the migration of VMs. Content migration to Kubernetes is also being progressed, however there is a resourcing risk associated with achieving this.
06/07/26 ProtonVPN has been onboarded. Ticket for Proton VPN subscription -        to action set up of Proton VPN.

items.summary: Kodex - Coordinate creation of Jira space for Kodex AI team

panels.text: Following the call with Claus, George created a Miro board outlining the proposed workflow:  Kodex AI - Miro .  Awaiting the confirmation from George the design can be passed to TechOps team for creation in Jira.
06/07/2026 Organising NDA with legal / prescient security.  Have requested new quote.

6/07/2026 TDD sent to Cabot in the hope it answers their questions. Waiting to hear but David has been on leave. Main actions: any flexibility on deadline of end of March? Document what front end of process does - scanners, workflow, etc. I have said that we will provide this by 24th of July, if not before
Mary Leung’s and Melissa Pezzotti’s TR devices have been returned.

Migration of the mailboxes has been fully completed. Issues with the email forwarding is being addresses. Temporal solution has been implemented by Stefan. Awaiting feedback from TR for long-term solution.  Distribution lists have been recreated by Service Desk Next phase: OneDrive migration to be kicked-off in August. O365 migration plan:

Dipak requested a quote from the local vendor for the monitors. [ Open ] Dipak has provided the age details of the devices being replaced. The next step is to coordinate the collection and secure wiping of the old laptops in India and the USA. Softcat responded that they can support us with the secure wiping process as ISO cert. in India region. I’ve requested the quote for that work. Awaiting their feedback. [ Open ] Dipak has provided the current M365 licensing inventory. Following a review of the inventory, it has been determined that the existing licenses should be transitioned to E2 licenses. [ Open ]

List of the business systems under review     [ Open ] PyCharm licenses - Dipak checked and confirmed PyCharm needs to be used. Side by side comparison between PyCharm and VS Code has been created by Dipak:   . The number of free PyCharm license available for allocation: 7. Dipak will confirm the users they should be allocated to.  [ Open ] Postman licenses in progress (Awaiting feedback from Barry)  [ Open ] Migration of 4CRisk Jira has been completed on migration Sandbox. A follow-up session was held today with Dipak and George to review and agree on the Jira workflow for the 4CRisk space within the CUBE instance. George has created a Miro board and will prepare the workflow design based on the outcomes and decisions from today's meeting. [ Open ] DNS migration, Faaris spoke with 4crisk team last Thursday and confirmed plan of action ready to go for migrating  4crisk.ai . There are other domains on their portfolio which their team is looking into confirming where the DNS records sit before we can proceed with those domains. Tracked under epic    and task     [ Open ] To replace the existing 4CRisk OpenAI account and Gemini API integrations, a dedicated project space needs to be created in AI Foundry. Dipak confirmed project space has been created in AI Foundry and the team is working on this. By the end of the week they will come to conclusion.[ Open ]

11/06/2026 Spreadsheet now shows colour coding for date of action or date to be actioned Customers identified as Inform Now have been notified. A few more are due now but next batch aren’t due until July
20/01/26 Dev and Test databases upgraded to SQL 2022. UAT to be upgraded in January and Prod in February.
